frame_analyses.description: Two hands in orange gloves visible with purple and yellow suture strands. Suture block (yellow foam) visible at top. This is instructional content labeled 'Meltzer's knot' with decorative overlay.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational demonstration setup. No actual FLS trainer visible.

frame_analyses.description: Both hands forming initial knot loop with purple and yellow suture. Hands working extracorporeally outside the suture block.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating knot configuration technique. Clear hand positioning for educational purposes.

frame_analyses.description: Hands manipulating suture to form loop structure. Purple strand being threaded through yellow strand configuration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Step-by-step knot tying demonstration with clear visibility of hand mechanics.

frame_analyses.description: Completing loop formation. Hands positioned to demonstrate proper knot configuration before cinching.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational overlay visible. Demonstrating proper loop orientation and hand placement.

frame_analyses.description: Second 'Meltzer's knot' overlay appears. Hands repositioning to show knot tensioning technique.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition to tension application phase of demonstration.

frame_analyses.description: Hands applying tension to complete knot. Both suture ends being controlled. Knot appears to be cinching down.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating proper tension application and cinching mechanics. Clear hand control visible.

frame_analyses.description: Final knot configuration visible. Both suture tails hanging freely below completed knot. Knot appears secure and properly formed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final configuration shows well-formed knot. Educational demonstration complete for knot tying technique.

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot visible with both suture ends controlled by hands. Knot positioned at appropriate tension.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating final knot security and tail management.

video_classification_rationale: This video is classified as INSTRUCTIONAL, not performance. Evidence: (1) Large decorative overlay banners labeled 'Meltzer's knot' appear multiple times across the video; (2) Suture block is visible as a simple foam model, not the actual FLS trainer equipment with Penrose drain and marked targets; (3) No actual FLS task is being executed—only extracorporeal knot-tying technique is demonstrated; (4) Camera angle and setup are clearly didactic/educational rather than performative; (5) No timing clock, no penalty tracking, no actual task completion criteria (suture through marks, drain closure assessment, suture cutting) are present.
cannot_score_rationale: This is not a scorable FLS task4 performance. It is purely educational content demonstrating knot-tying technique. The video does not show: (1) Actual FLS trainer with Penrose drain and marked targets, (2) Suture passing through marked entry/exit points, (3) Drain slit closure assessment, (4) Suture cutting phase inside trainer, (5) Any element of task completion timing or penalty tracking. Scoring would be invalid without these task-critical elements.

demonstrated_techniques: Extracorporeal knot formation with two-suture configuration
Loop creation and orientation for knot tying
Proper knot configuration (Meltzer's knot variant shown)
Bilateral hand control during knot formation
verbal_cues: Meltzer's knot (named overlay cue visible in frames)
common_errors_addressed: Proper loop orientation to ensure secure knot
Balanced tension application on both strands
Hand positioning to prevent knot slippage

summary: This is an educational instructional video demonstrating extracorporeal knot-tying technique, specifically the Meltzer's knot variant. It is NOT a recorded FLS task4 performance and therefore cannot be scored using the FLS rubric. The video effectively demonstrates proper hand mechanics and knot-formation technique for educational purposes but lacks all task-specific elements (actual trainer, marked targets, drain assessment, task completion criteria) required for valid FLS scoring.
